_comment: Maps a PR author's GitHub username to the path prefixes they may touch. An author WITHOUT an entry here is unrestricted. Enforced by .github/workflows/path-restriction.yml on every PR.
_current_limitation: Only two real identities exist today: jnmrclmbsjse (human — runs PO, Tech Lead, QA, UI/UX) and jnmrclmbsjse-dev-bot (Dev agent). Because one account serves several roles, the per-role rows from the least-privilege table (QA -> e2e/ only, UI/UX -> docs/design/ only) CANNOT be enforced by author: restricting jnmrclmbsjse to e2e/ would break its Tech Lead and PO work. Those rows remain policy-only, living in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, until per-role GitHub App identities exist. Only the Dev boundary below is actually enforced.
_dev_bot_rationale: Dev gets broad codebase write but is explicitly BLOCKED from docs/design/ (UI/UX owns design tokens and mockups) and docs/adr/ (Tech Lead owns architecture decisions). Dev consumes both read-only per AGENTS.md; this check makes that boundary real rather than prompt-trust. If a legitimate Dev PR is blocked by this list, add the missing path here rather than removing the restriction.
